overview.windEstimatedTitle: Wind inferred from GPS tracks: Stormglass not available for this session
overview.windEstimatedDescription: Stormglass was not available for this session: the wind direction (TWD) and all derived metrics — points of sail, TWA, VMG — are inferred from heuristics on your GPS tracks, not from satellite observation. Expect a larger margin of error.

exportModal.title: Export PDF report
exportModal.subtitle: Configure the parameters before generating the document. Data will be filtered to the currently selected time window.
exportModal.flyThresholdHelp: SOG above which the rider is considered foiling. Default {{value}} kts.

exportModal.shortPeriodWarningBody: you selected less than {{seconds}} seconds. Aggregate metrics (VMG, % foiling, maneuver percentiles) will not be very meaningful.

polar.howToReadTitle: How to read the polar chart
polar.howToReadIntro: The polar chart shows your speed (SOG) as a function of the angle to the wind (TWA).
polar.howToReadAxis: 0° at the top is the wind direction; the farther from the centre, the faster.
polar.howToReadColors: Colours separate Upwind, Beam reach and Broad/Downwind.
polar.howToReadP90: The P90 curve highlights the top edge of performance: the fastest 10% for each TWA.

polarView.helpAria: How to read the polar chart
polarView.helpTitle: How to read the polar chart
polarView.helpP1: The polar chart shows the athlete's performance at every angle to the wind. The wind blows from the top to the bottom of the chart (0° = directly upwind, 180° = directly downwind).
polarView.helpP2: The radial axis represents speed: the farther a point is from the centre, the faster the boat was at that angle.
polarView.helpP3: The gold curve (P90) marks sustainable peak performance: for each angle, the speed reached in 90% of cases. It's the reference curve to assess the athlete's potential.
polarView.helpP4: The grey line is the mean: the typical speed at that angle — useful to gauge consistency.
polarView.helpP5: Translucent grey dots are every real sample of the session — they hint at the spread.
polarView.helpP6: To optimise performance: the upwind angle where VMG peaks (typically 40°-50°) and the downwind angle where downwind VMG peaks (typically 140°-150°).
polarView.interpretationNote: Polar quality depends on TWD estimation accuracy. With shifty wind, interpret with caution.
polarView.intervalTooShort: Selected interval is short ({{minutes}} min): the polar may not be representative.
polarView.insufficientDataBody: {{count}} valid points in the selected window (at least {{min}} required). Pick a wider interval.

flyThreshold.label: Fly threshold
flyThreshold.tooltip: Speed above which the rider is considered foiling.

glossary.title: Glossary · Terms and abbreviations
glossary.subtitle: Quick reference for the metrics and concepts used in Varea

twd.name: True Wind Direction
twd.description: Direction the true wind blows from, in degrees (0°=North, 90°=East, 180°=South, 270°=West). In Varea it can be estimated from GPS by analyzing course distribution, or taken from the Stormglass API when available.

tws.name: True Wind Speed
tws.description: Strength of the true wind in knots (kts): how hard it is blowing. In Varea it comes from Stormglass when available, otherwise it is not computed.

twa.name: True Wind Angle
twa.description: Angle between the boat's course (COG) and the wind direction (TWD). Range 0°-180°. It determines the point of sail: low TWA = upwind, medium TWA = beam reach, high TWA = run.

sog.name: Speed Over Ground
sog.description: Boat speed relative to the ground, measured by GPS in knots. It is the "true" speed of the craft, independent of currents.

cog.name: Course Over Ground
cog.description: Direction of the boat's motion relative to North, in degrees. On many Garmin GPS watches this value is missing or zero — in that case Varea reconstructs it from consecutive GPS points via spherical bearing calculation.

vmg.name: Velocity Made Good
vmg.description: Component of SOG along the wind direction. Formula: VMG = SOG × cos(TWA). It measures how effectively the rider progresses toward the windward mark (upwind) or away from it (downwind). Two riders with similar SOG can have very different VMG if they sail at different angles.

bolina.name: Upwind
bolina.description: Upwind point of sail, TWA typically 30°-60°. The boat climbs toward the wind. It is the most technical point of sail and where VMG matters most.

traverso.name: Beam reach
traverso.description: Wind on the beam, TWA 60°-120°. Usually the fastest point of sail overall.

virata.name: Tack
virata.description: Upwind maneuver: the boat brings the bow through the wind, switching tack.

strambata.name: Gybe
strambata.description: Downwind maneuver: the boat brings the stern through the wind, switching tack.

vIn.name: Entry speed
vIn.description: SOG in the seconds before the maneuver. Shows how much speed the rider carries into the tack or gybe.

vMin.name: Minimum speed
vMin.description: Lowest SOG reached during the maneuver. Measures how much speed is "lost" in the transition.

vOut.name: Exit speed
vOut.description: SOG in the seconds after the maneuver, once stabilized.

deltaV.name: Delta-V
deltaV.description: Difference between V.OUT and V.IN. Positive = the maneuver left the rider faster; negative = speed was lost. Key metric to assess maneuver quality.

ttr.name: Time To Recovery 50%
ttr.description: Seconds needed to recover 50% of the speed lost during the maneuver. Lower = more efficient maneuver.

fly.name: FLY
fly.description: State in which the board is flying on the hydrofoil, with SOG above the configured threshold (default 12 kts). A "fly" maneuver is a tack or gybe executed while staying on the foil.

touch.name: TOUCH
touch.description: State in which the board touches the water, with SOG below the foiling threshold. A "touch" maneuver means the rider lost flight during execution.

soglia.name: Foiling threshold
soglia.description: SOG (user-configurable) above which Varea considers the rider to be flying. Default: 12 kts. Editable in the Maneuvers and Lab views.

foilingRatio.name: Foiling ratio
foilingRatio.description: Percentage of time SOG stayed above the foiling threshold. Shows how much of the session was actually on the foil.

stormglass.name: Stormglass
stormglass.description: API service that provides historical wind data (TWD, TWS), currents, and waves for specific geographic coordinates and dates. When available, Varea uses it as the primary wind source. The green "FROM STORMGLASS" pill indicates that the data comes from the API.

hz.name: 1 Hz
hz.description: GPS data sampling frequency: one point per second. All data in Varea is resampled to 1 Hz after parsing the original FIT file.

fit.name: FIT
fit.description: Garmin standard binary file format for recording sports activities. Contains GPS points, timestamps, and session metadata.

pdf.aggregateDesc: Average and peak speed, time spent foiling, VMG and breakdown of points of sail over the period.
pdf.maneuverAnalysisDesc: One row per maneuver in the period. Green = delta-v better than 75th percentile, red = worse than 25th.
pdf.noManeuversInPeriod: No maneuvers detected in the selected period.
pdf.deltaSummary: Delta-v summary: mean {{mean}} kts  ·  std dev {{std}} kts  ·  {{count}} maneuvers
pdf.gpsTrackDesc: Period path, coloured by point of sail. Green dot = start, red = end.
pdf.coachNotesDesc: Time-stamped annotations on the key moments of the selected period.
pdf.startAnalysisDesc: Athlete state at the gun and time needed to stabilize foiling.
pdf.thresholdNotReached: threshold never reached in the period

errors.pdfGenerationFailed: PDF generation failed. Check the console for details.
